criteria: Cache size detector correctly identifies L1, L2, and L3 cache boundaries by measuring latency inflection points across working set sizes from 4KB to 256MB
Latency measurements use pointer-chasing technique with LCG-based permutation that defeats hardware prefetchers to measure true memory access latency
Sequential access benchmark demonstrates at least 3x faster throughput than random access for arrays exceeding L1 cache size (1M+ elements)
Stride benchmark shows constant access latency for strides up to 64 bytes then measurable latency increase for larger strides, correctly detecting cache line size
Hardware performance counter integration via perf_event_open collects L1-dcache-load-misses and LLC-load-misses for each benchmark scenario
All measurements include warmup iterations (minimum 1000 configurable) before timed measurement to eliminate page fault and cold cache effects
Benchmarks run multiple samples (minimum 10 configurable) with statistical reporting including mean, standard deviation, min, max, and median
Results documented with formatted tables showing latency transitions at cache boundaries with percentage change annotations
Compiler barriers (asm volatile with memory clobber) prevent dead code elimination of benchmark loops and force actual memory reads
LatencyResult structure includes outlier_count field identifying samples more than 3 standard deviations from mean
Pointer chase uses aligned_alloc with 64-byte cache line alignment for buffer allocation
PerfCounterGroup handles permission denied gracefully with fallback to time-only measurement and clear user instructions
rdtsc_serialized function uses cpuid as serializing instruction for cycle-accurate timing where needed
CacheInfo structure includes detection_reliable boolean flag indicating whether measurements had acceptable variance
